notes: Source: banksbd.org / DBBL website 2025. FDR: 3mo=7.00%, 6mo=7.10%, 12mo=7.20%. Premature allowed with penalty.
Source: DBBL 2024. Quarterly/half-yearly interest payout option. Premature allowed with penalty.
Source: DBBL 2024. 7-day notice required for withdrawal. Good for emergency fund.
Source: banksbd.org / BRAC Bank 2025. FDR: 3mo=5.81%, 12mo=6.00%. Various tenure options.
Source: BRAC Bank 2024. Various tenure options. Interest credited monthly/quarterly.
Source: BRAC Bank 2024. Profit multiplied at maturity. Higher lock-in.
Source: IBBL Provisional Profit Rates w.e.f 01.01.2026. MTDR: 3mo=9.50%, 12mo=10.00%, 24mo=10.20%, 36mo=10.50%.
Source: IBBL 2024. Halal investment. Profit-loss sharing; profit not guaranteed.
Source: IBBL 2024. Dedicated for Hajj savings. Long-term, Shariah-compliant.
Source: Bangladesh Bank 2024. Highest safe return. Interest quarterly. TDS 10% if >5L.
Source: Bangladesh Bank 2024. Interest paid every 3 months. Same tax rule.
Source: Bangladesh Post Office 2024. Govt-backed. Suitable for rural users.
Source: Sonali Bank PLC official circular, 2025. Govt-backed DPS. Tax rebate eligible.
Source: Sonali Bank PLC Head Office Circular No-42, Nov 2024. FDR 3-6mo=7.25%, 6mo-1yr=7.50%, 1yr-3yr=7.75%. Premature withdrawal allowed with penalty.
Source: Sonali Bank PLC circular, effective 01 Sep 2025. Revised rate from 2.75% to 2.50%.
Source: Janata Bank PLC website 2025. State-owned bank; stable returns. Premature closure with penalty.
Source: Janata Bank PLC 2025. Monthly installment savings scheme. Govt guarantee.
Source: Janata Bank PLC 2025. Regular savings account. No minimum balance required for some categories.
Source: banksbd.org / Agrani Bank PLC 2025. FDR: 3mo=8.50%, 6mo=8.75%, 12mo=9.00%. Govt-owned bank.
Source: Agrani Bank PLC 2025. Monthly savings scheme. Govt-backed. Tax certificate eligible.
Source: Agrani Bank PLC 2025. Widely available across Bangladesh. 1,200+ branches.
Source: banksbd.org / Rupali Bank PLC 2025. FDR all tenors = 5.00%. Listed bank on DSE.
Source: Rupali Bank PLC 2025. Monthly savings plan. Govt-owned.
Source: Rupali Bank PLC 2025. Largest govt bank listed on DSE. Wide rural branch network.
Source: banksbd.org / Pubali Bank PLC 2025. FDR: 3mo=8.75%, 6mo=9.00%, 12mo=9.25%.
Source: Pubali Bank PLC 2025. Flexible savings plan up to 9.50%. Recognized as Top 10 Sustainable Bank.
Source: Pubali Bank PLC / arthikalap.com 2026. Senior Citizen Deposit at Benchmark+1%. Best in market for retirees.
Source: banksbd.org / Prime Bank PLC 2025. FDR: 3mo=7.00%, 6mo=9.00%, 12mo=10.50%. Strong stability.
Source: Prime Bank PLC 2025. DPS 9.00–9.50%. Digital nano-loan eligible after 1 yr tenure.
Source: Prime Bank PLC 2025. Regular savings with digital banking via Prime Bank app.
Source: banksbd.org / Mercantile Bank PLC 2025. FDR: 3mo=7.50%, 6mo=8.00%, 12mo=9.00%, 36mo=10.50%.
Source: Mercantile Bank PLC 2025. Monthly savings plan. 146 branches nationwide.
Source: Mercantile Bank PLC 2025. Regular savings with ATM access.
Source: IBBL Provisional Profit Rates w.e.f 01.01.2026. MMPDS 5yr=11.50%, 3yr=10.50%. Monthly profit payout. Shariah-compliant.

scheme_name: IBBL Senior Citizen Monthly Profit Scheme (MSCMPDS)
notes: Source: IBBL Provisional Profit Rates w.e.f 01.01.2026. MSCMPDS: 5yr=11.70%, 3yr=10.60%, 1yr=10.00%. Best for retirees seeking monthly income.
Source: IBBL Provisional Profit Rates w.e.f 01.01.2026. NRB Bond: 5yr=7.30%, 10yr=8.00%. For non-resident Bangladeshis.

Source: National Savings Directorate (NSD) 2025. Rate 11.52% p.a. quarterly payout. Only for women, retired govt employees & senior citizens (65+). Max investment 45 lakh. TDS 10% if >5 lakh invested. Best for: Women / Retired.

Source: NSD / Bangladesh Bank 2025. Rate 11.76% p.a. highest NSD rate. Exclusively for non-resident Bangladeshis (NRBs) / expat workers. Quarterly interest payout. Can be encashed after 1 year. Foreign currency or BDT via banking channel. Best for: Expat Workers.

Source: bKash official app / BRAC Bank MFS 2025. Interest 4.5% p.a. on savings balance. Anytime withdrawal no lock-in. Accessible via mobile app nationwide. Ideal for low-income, rural, and unbanked users. No minimum balance penalty. Best for: Low income / Mobile users.

Source: ICB Bangladesh (Investment Corporation of Bangladesh) 2025. Historical return approx 1015% p.a. (not guaranteed). Govt-managed diversified fund investing in DSE-listed equities & bonds. Dividend-bearing. Can be sold on secondary market. Suitable for middle-income long-term investors. Best for: Middle+ / Long-term. *Approx historical return. Actual returns may vary.

Source: Dhaka Stock Exchange (DSE) / BSEC 2025. Returns highly variable depends on stock selection and market conditions. Requires BO (Beneficiary Owner) account via a TREC broker. Anytime buy/sell during trading hours. Capital loss possible. Best for experienced investors with higher risk appetite. Best for: Experienced / Upper income. *Not financial advice.

Source: Chittagong Stock Exchange (CSE) / BSEC 2025. Second major bourse in Bangladesh. Requires BO account via CSE-registered broker. Returns volatile and dependent on market conditions. Capital loss possible. Smaller liquidity than DSE for some scrips. Best for: Experienced / Upper income. *Not financial advice.

Source: BSEC / DSE SME Board 2025. Stocks of small & medium enterprises listed on DSE SME board. Higher growth potential but very low liquidity and high volatility. Eligible for SME-focused investors. Circuit breaker rules differ from main board. Best for: High-risk tolerant / Experienced. *Not financial advice.

provider: Various AMCs (e.g., ICB AMCL, AIMS, LankaBangla)
notes: Source: BSEC / respective AMC prospectuses 2025. Open-end funds redeemable at NAV. Primarily invested in DSE/CSE-listed equities. Returns not guaranteed; NAV fluctuates with market. Managed by BSEC-licensed Asset Management Companies. Best for: SIP-style equity exposure. *Not financial advice.

Source: BSEC / DSE 2025. Closed-end funds traded on DSE at market price (may trade at premium or discount to NAV). Annual dividend payouts from underlying equity/bond portfolio. Market price risk plus portfolio risk. Best for: Dividend-seeking equity investors. *Not financial advice.

Source: BSEC IPO Regulations 2025. Apply for newly listed company shares via banker or broker. Lottery-based allotment for oversubscribed IPOs. Lock-in applies for some categories. Post-listing price may fall below issue price. Lottery allotment not guaranteed. Best for: Market-savvy investors. *Not financial advice.

provider: Private Real Estate Market (Dhaka, Chattogram etc.)
notes: Source: REHAB / Property market surveys 2025. Capital appreciation potential 520% p.a. in prime areas. High entry cost, low liquidity, transaction costs 812% (registration, tax, legal). Subject to regulatory, legal title, and developer default risks. Rental yield 46% p.a. in Dhaka. Best for: High-net-worth / Long-term. *Not financial advice.

Source: BSEC REIT Rules 2025 / Shanta REIT prospectus. REITs invest in income-generating real estate. Traded on DSE; price subject to market fluctuation. Dividends from rental income. Regulatory framework still maturing in Bangladesh. Best for: Real-estate exposure without physical purchase. *Not financial advice.

Source: Startup Bangladesh / BASIS / Venture capital industry reports 2025. Equity stake in early-stage Bangladeshi startups. Very high upside potential but majority of startups fail. Illiquid until exit (acquisition, IPO). Requires due diligence, deal negotiation. Typically for accredited/HNW investors. Best for: High-risk tolerant HNW investors. *Not financial advice.

provider: Various Private Banks (e.g., BRAC, DBBL, EBL)
notes: Source: Bangladesh Bank FX regulations / bank circulars 2025. USD/EUR/GBP savings account for eligible persons (NRBs, exporters, remitters). Interest 1.54% p.a. in foreign currency. Exchange rate risk: BDT/USD fluctuation can erode or boost returns. Regulated by Bangladesh Bank. Repatriation rules apply. Best for: NRBs / Exporters / Forex-hedged savers. *Not financial advice.

Source: Eastern Bank Limited (EBL) product brochure 2025. Lump-sum deposit doubles at maturity (~78 years). Effective CAGR ~9.510.5%. Premature encashment allowed with reduced rate penalty. No interim payout full amount received at maturity. Medium risk due to long lock-in and opportunity cost. Best for: Long-term lump-sum savers targeting wealth doubling. *Not financial advice.

Source: The City Bank PLC rate sheet 2025. High-yield FDR tier for large deposits (1 lakh+). 3yr=10.00%, 5yr=11.50%. Quarterly/half-yearly interest payout option. Renewal at prevailing rate. Medium risk due to 35yr lock-in; premature exit incurs rate penalty. Best for: High-balance long-term depositors. *Not financial advice.

Source: Southeast Bank PLC DPS product sheet 2025. Monthly installment DPS targeting 10 lakh accumulation. Rate 10.0011.00% p.a. depending on tenure. 5yr (60 installments) at 13,000/month yields ~10L. Medium risk because of long commitment period and reinvestment rate uncertainty. Premature closure with 2% rate reduction. Best for: Salaried workers / wealth builders. *Not financial advice.

Source: Social Islami Bank (SIBL) product circular 2025. Shariah-compliant Mudaraba deposit that doubles the principal over 7 years (effective ~10.5% CAGR). Profit-loss sharing basis; profit not contractually guaranteed but historically consistent. No interim payout. Encashable after 2 years with proportionate profit. Medium risk: long lock-in + profit uncertainty under Islamic banking. Best for: Shariah-conscious long-term savers. *Not financial advice.

scheme_name: Bangladesh Govt Treasury Bond (5–10yr, Secondary Market)
notes: Source: Bangladesh Bank Debt Management / DSE Bond Board 2025. Government-issued treasury bonds (5yr & 10yr) purchased via primary dealer banks or the DSE bond market. Coupon 10.5012.50% p.a. (semi-annual). Sovereign guarantee on coupon & principal; medium risk arises from interest rate risk if sold before maturity on secondary market bond price moves inversely to interest rates. TDS 5% on interest. Best for: Institutional / HNW fixed-income investors. *Secondary market price risk applies. Not financial advice.

provider: ICB Asset Management Company Ltd (ICB AMCL)
notes: Source: ICB AMCL fund factsheet 2025. Open-end balanced mutual fund managed by govt-backed ICB AMCL. Invests in a mix of DSE-listed equities (~60%) and fixed-income instruments (~40%). Historical dividend yield 814% p.a. NAV fluctuates with equity market. Redeemable at NAV with 15 business day settlement. Lower risk than pure equity funds; medium risk due to partial equity exposure. Best for: SIP investors / retirement savings / middle income. *Past performance not indicative of future. Not financial advice.

scheme_name: Delta Life Insurance Endowment Savings Plan
notes: Source: Delta Life Insurance Company Limited product guide 2025. Endowment life insurance policy with savings component. Pays a guaranteed sum assured + bonuses at maturity or to nominees on death. Effective return 710% p.a. depending on bonus declaration. Premiums eligible for tax rebate under Section 44. Surrendering early incurs significant loss. Regulated by Insurance Development & Regulatory Authority (IDRA). Medium risk: long commitment, bonus not guaranteed, insurer credit risk. Best for: Family protection + long-term savings. *Not financial advice.

Source: Jiban Bima Corporation (JBC) / IDRA 2025. State-owned life insurer offering endowment & term plans. Guaranteed sum assured + declared bonuses at maturity. Historically conservative bonus ~79% p.a. effective return. Death benefit provides family protection. Premium tax rebate eligible. Policy loans available after 3 years. Govt backing reduces insolvency risk vs private insurers. Medium risk due to very long lock-in and opportunity cost. Best for: Conservative long-term savers / breadwinners. *Not financial advice.

scheme_name: NCC Bank Double Benefit Deposit Scheme
notes: Source: NCC Bank PLC product brochure 2025. Lump-sum deposit scheme that doubles invested amount at maturity over approximately 78 years (effective CAGR ~9.510.0%). No interim interest payment. Premature encashment allowed after 1 year at reduced rate. Medium risk primarily due to long lock-in period and reinvestment rate risk. Bank has 120+ branches nationwide. Best for: Medium-term wealth-doubling objective. *Not financial advice.

scheme_name: Mutual Trust Bank (MTB) Special Term Deposit
notes: Source: Mutual Trust Bank PLC rate card 2025. High-yield fixed deposit for tenures of 35 years. 3yr=10.00%, 5yr=11.25% p.a. Interest credited quarterly. Premature closure allowed with 1.5% rate penalty. MTB is a BSEC-listed private bank with strong SME banking focus. Medium risk due to 35yr lock-in and private bank credit risk. Best for: Medium-to-long-term fixed-income investors seeking above-benchmark yields. *Not financial advice.

Source: Global crypto market / Bangladesh Bank warnings 2025. Cryptocurrency trading is NOT regulated or legally permitted in Bangladesh under Bangladesh Bank FX regulations. Bangladesh Bank has issued circulars prohibiting crypto transactions. Extreme price volatility; potential for 100% capital loss. Listed here for informational/risk-profiling purposes only. Best for: INFORMATIONAL ONLY not recommended in Bangladesh. *High legal & financial risk.

Source: Bangladesh Jewellers Association (BAJUS) / gold price history 2025. Physical gold/silver purchase via BAJUS-registered jewellers. Price linked to global USD gold price + BDT/USD exchange rate. No formal commodity exchange in Bangladesh; traded informally. Making charges 815% of purchase. Storage risk. Best for: Inflation hedge / long-term. *Price fluctuation risk. Not financial advice.

provider: Various fintech platforms (e.g., bKash Gold, bank gold schemes)
notes: Source: Fintech industry / BAJUS / BB guidelines 2025. Fractional digital gold savings via mobile or banking apps. Value tracks global gold price in BDT. Platform/counterparty risk. No physical delivery in most schemes. Regulatory framework for digital gold in Bangladesh is still developing. Best for: Small investors wanting gold exposure. *Price & platform risk. Not financial advice.

Source: BSEC / DSE bond market / corporate prospectuses 2025. Fixed-coupon bonds issued by private corporates; Sukuk for Shariah-compliant issuers. Coupon 914% p.a. Credit/default risk of the issuer. Secondary market illiquid. Rated by ECRL/CRISL. Higher yield than govt bonds but no sovereign guarantee. Best for: Yield-seeking / Institutional. *Credit risk applies. Not financial advice.

provider: Emerging BD Fintech Platforms (e.g., local P2P portals)
notes: Source: Bangladesh Bank fintech sandbox / industry reports 2025. Peer-to-peer lending via digital platforms connecting borrowers and lenders. Returns 1220% p.a. but subject to borrower default risk, platform insolvency risk, and limited regulatory protection. P2P lending regulatory framework in Bangladesh is nascent. Capital is NOT guaranteed. Best for: Risk-tolerant tech-savvy investors. *Very high risk. Not financial advice.

Source: BSEC Margin Rules / DSE broker circulars 2025. Leveraged stock trading using broker-provided margin loan (typically 1:1 to 1:1.5 leverage). Amplifies both gains and losses. Forced margin calls if portfolio value drops. Loan interest 1014% p.a. charged by broker. Can result in total capital loss and outstanding debt. Suitable ONLY for very experienced traders. Best for: Professional traders only. *Extreme risk. Not financial advice.
